summary: Joel announces that the Joel on Software discussion groups are now running on an unreleased pre-alpha version of FogBugz 6.0, using the community as a real-world stress test. The groups have always been powered by FogBugz, and the team wants to test new features under heavy load before wider release. Planned features include user account registration with visual indicators and read/unread post tracking. Joel acknowledges the community is essentially serving as guinea pigs for data-intensive features that need validation at scale before reaching official beta testers.
key_points: The Joel on Software discussion groups serve double duty as both community forums and a real-world testing ground for FogBugz
FogBugz 6.0 introduces user accounts with visual indicators and server-side read/unread tracking to replace browser-based history
Features are being rolled out incrementally to manage risk, starting with cosmetic changes before enabling data-intensive functionality
Using a large existing user base for load testing before beta is a pragmatic approach to validating scalability concerns
money_quotes: The four main discussion groups on this site are implemented with FogBugz, my company's flagship project management system.
We've been developing 6.0 for a while now and want to do some testing of it under heavy load.
This is one of the scarier features since it's kind of data-intensive for very large numbers of users.
Which is why we're using you as guinea pigs before we inflict it on our beta testers.
key_insight: Using your own active community as a pre-beta stress test for new features is a practical way to validate scalability before wider release.
